title: Aether — a language in your browser
description: A from-scratch ML-family programming language with Hindley–Milner inference, a kind system with higher-kinded types — genuine Functor/Applicative/Monad classes with superclasses — and THREE BACKENDS that all run the same typed program byte-for-byte: a bytecode stack VM, a JavaScript code generator, and a NATIVE WEBASSEMBLY BACKEND that hand-assembles the program into a real `.wasm` module (a from-scratch binary encoder — no wabt, no binaryen), instantiates it in the browser, and runs it — closures via `call_indirect` over a bump-allocator heap, constant-space TAIL CALLS (return_call), structural `match`, and ADTs/records/tuples as tagged cells, with printing/show/comparison delegated to imports that reuse the VM's own code so equivalence holds by construction. The WebAssembly backend is SELF-DESCRIBING: a `name` section lets it DISASSEMBLE ITS OWN BYTES back into readable WAT text via a from-scratch binary decoder (the mirror of the encoder — no wabt, no wasm2wat) that resolves every call/global/local to a `$name`, and a shared SMALL-INTEGER CACHE lets the bump allocator reuse cells instead of boxing fresh ones (invisible to results since every value is compared structurally), with the module counting the allocations it saved. You can download the `.wasm` and run it anywhere. Plus DERIVING `(Eq, Ord, Show, Enum, Bounded, Functor, Foldable)` synthesised from a data type's shape, property-based testing that generates inputs from inferred types and shrinks failures, monadic do-notation, list comprehensions, an interactive HM derivation tree, a time-travel debugger, and turtle graphics.
